rules_enforced: Rule 1: No fact_base.json imports or reads
Rule 2: data_source must be dcl or live (demo = FAIL)
Rule 3: No environmental excuses — every test runs
Rule 4: HTTP only — no internal function calls

actual.name: Above-market exec compensation — Meridian
actual.support_reference: Executive compensation analysis vs. industry benchmarks
actual.rationale: Meridian CEO/CFO compensation $18M above market median for comparable firms.

actual.name: Above-market exec compensation — Cascadia
actual.support_reference: Executive compensation analysis vs. industry benchmarks
actual.rationale: Cascadia founder/CEO compensation $8M above market median.

actual.name: Non-recurring: M litigation reserve
actual.support_reference: Meridian 10-K Note 14 — Litigation contingencies
actual.rationale: One-time litigation reserve for patent infringement case settled in Q3.

actual.name: Non-recurring: C delivery center closure
actual.support_reference: Cascadia 10-K Note 8 — Restructuring charges
actual.rationale: One-time charge for Manila delivery center closure and staff transition.

actual.name: Transaction costs — this deal (Meridian)
actual.support_reference: Merger proxy statement — Transaction expenses
actual.rationale: Meridian-side investment banking, legal, and accounting fees for this transaction.

actual.name: Transaction costs — this deal (Cascadia)
actual.support_reference: Merger proxy statement — Transaction expenses
actual.rationale: Cascadia-side advisory and legal fees for this transaction.

actual.name: Related-party leases — C founder properties
actual.support_reference: Cascadia related-party transaction disclosures
actual.rationale: Cascadia leases 2 properties from founder entity at above-market rates; $4M annual excess.

actual.name: Run-rate: new contracts not at full revenue (Meridian)
actual.support_reference: Meridian backlog analysis — contracts signed but not fully ramped
actual.rationale: 3 large contracts signed in Q3-Q4 not yet at full run-rate revenue.

actual.name: Run-rate: new contracts not at full revenue (Cascadia)
actual.support_reference: Cascadia backlog analysis — contracts signed but not fully ramped
actual.rationale: 2 BPM engagements ramping to full headcount in Q1-Q2 2026.

actual.name: Utilization normalization — M Q2-Q3 dip
actual.support_reference: Meridian utilization reports — Q2/Q3 2025 vs. trailing 8-quarter avg
actual.rationale: Q2-Q3 utilization dipped to 73% vs. normalized 78%; add-back for temporary bench buildup.

actual.name: Offshore labor mix shift — C in progress
actual.support_reference: Cascadia workforce planning — offshore migration program
actual.rationale: Cascadia migrating 15% of onshore delivery to offshore; $12M annualized savings at completion.

actual.name: C attrition normalization 18% → 15%
actual.support_reference: Cascadia HR metrics — attrition trending down from 18% to 15% target
actual.rationale: Cascadia attrition at 18% vs. 15% industry norm; excess recruiting/training cost add-back.

actual.name: Bench optimization — consulting
actual.support_reference: Bench utilization analysis — 4500 consultants × cross-deploy model
actual.rationale: Cross-deploying Meridian Partners bench consultants onto Cascadia Process Solutions delivery engagements.

actual.name: Bench optimization — delivery
actual.support_reference: Delivery bench analysis — 4200 FTEs × cross-deploy model
actual.rationale: Redeploying idle Cascadia Process Solutions delivery FTEs onto Meridian Partners project support roles.

actual.name: Corporate function consolidation
actual.support_reference: People overlap analysis — Finance, HR, IT, Legal functions
actual.rationale: Consolidating overlapping corporate functions; reduction based on min(M,C) headcount per function.

actual.name: Vendor consolidation
actual.support_reference: Vendor overlap analysis — 170 overlapping vendors with consolidation savings
actual.rationale: Consolidating overlapping vendor contracts for volume discounts and eliminated redundancy.

actual.name: Technology redundancy elimination
actual.support_reference: Technology stack audit — overlapping SaaS, middleware, and dev tools
actual.rationale: Eliminating duplicate SaaS licenses, middleware, and internal tools post-combination.

actual.name: Cross-sell revenue contribution
actual.support_reference: Cross-sell pipeline — high-confidence ACV × capture rate × margin × ramp
actual.rationale: EBITDA contribution from cross-selling services to the other entity's non-overlapping clients.

actual.name: Integration costs Year 1
actual.support_reference: Integration management office budget — systems, people, branding
actual.rationale: Year 1 integration costs: IT system migration, org redesign, rebranding, change management.

actual.name: Retention packages
actual.support_reference: Retention program — key talent across both entities
actual.rationale: Year 1 retention bonuses for critical leadership and top performers.

results.query: Maestra engagement creation returns valid structure
Give me an overview of where we stand

assertions.actual:
Starting with the **cross-sell pipeline**, I found a $273M total pipeline with 103 named accounts. The high-confidence portion — 22 accounts with propensity scores above 75 — represents $119M in potential annual contract value.

The pipeline shows clear patterns: Meridian's Strategy Consulting maps well to Cascadia's healthcare and financial services clients, while Cascadia's F&A Outsourcing and Data Analytics BPO have strong demand from Meridian's technology sector clients.

Customer overlap analysis reveals **34 shared clients** representing $1.9B in combined revenue. Key concentrations include JPMorgan Chase ($97M combined), Microsoft ($98M), and UnitedHealth Group ($83M). Importantly, MegaCorp Global crosses our concentration threshold at $310M or 5.2% of the combined entity.

The **EBITDA bridge** shows the path from $1.2B reported EBITDA to $1.86B steady-state pro forma. Starting with entity adjustments of $228M — including executive compensation normalization, one-time charges, and run-rate adjustments — we get to $1.43B adjusted EBITDA.

Combination synergies add $430M, led by corporate function consolidation at $224M and bench optimization at $135M. After integration costs and retention packages, we reach $1.86B steady-state EBITDA.

**Vendor consolidation** offers significant savings with 170 overlapping vendors across cloud infrastructure, professional services, staffing, and technology categories. High-impact consolidations include AWS ($8.8M savings), Microsoft Azure ($6.5M), and PwC ($2.7M).

Looking at **quality of earnings**, the sustainability score is 78.9 (Grade B). Strong points include zero customer concentration risk and stable adjustments. Areas of concern include the high proportion of non-recurring revenue (83%) and short average contract duration impacting the score.

The analysis shows this is a fundamentally sound combination with substantial synergy opportunities, though execution will be critical given the dual-ERP challenge and significant integration scope.

Any specific area you'd like to explore further?

results.query: Walk me through the EBITDA bridge

assertions.actual:
The **EBITDA bridge** shows a path from $1.2B reported combined EBITDA to $1.86B steady-state pro forma — a 55% improvement.

Starting with reported EBITDA of $1.2B (Meridian's $1.35B minus Cascadia's $(131)M loss), we add $228M in entity adjustments to reach $1.43B adjusted EBITDA.

Key entity adjustments include:
- **$26M executive compensation normalization** — both entities running above market
- **$80M one-time charges** — Meridian's litigation reserve, Cascadia's delivery center closure, and transaction costs
- **$55M run-rate adjustments** — new contracts ramping plus Cascadia's offshore migration in progress
- **$63M utilization normalization** — Meridian's Q2-Q3 bench buildup and Cascadia's attrition trending down

The combination synergies add $430M total. **Corporate function consolidation** is the largest lever at $224M, followed by **bench optimization** at $135M where you cross-deploy consultants and delivery FTEs between practices.

**Vendor consolidation** contributes $49M from 170 overlapping vendors, while the **cross-sell contribution** adds $12M in EBITDA impact from high-confidence pipeline conversion.

Integration costs run $100M in Year 1 plus $25M in retention packages, giving you $1.73B Year 1 pro forma and $1.86B at steady-state.

The bridge assumes your 12.5x multiple, creating $4.6B in enterprise value upside from reported to steady-state.

Any specific adjustments or synergy assumptions you want to challenge?

results.query: What cross-sell opportunities do we have?

assertions.actual:
The **cross-sell pipeline** shows exceptional opportunity with **$273M total pipeline value across 103 named accounts**. The high-confidence portion — 22 accounts with propensity scores above 75 — represents **$119M in potential annual contract value**.

The pattern is clear: **Meridian to Cascadia** opportunities dominate at $155M, with Meridian's Strategy clients showing strong demand for Cascadia's F&A Outsourcing and Data & Analytics BPO services. **Cascadia to Meridian** adds $117M, primarily Strategy Consulting for Cascadia's BPM clients facing high process complexity.

Key highlights include Wavecrest Analytics 3 at $10M ACV (93 propensity score) and multiple $9M+ F&A Outsourcing opportunities from Meridian clients with 40+ manual processes and high outsourcing readiness scores.

The concentration risk is manageable — no single client exceeds 4% of total pipeline, though the top 10 opportunities represent 37% of high-confidence value.

Next area you'd like to explore?

results.query: Show me the quality of earnings analysis

assertions.actual:
The **quality of earnings** analysis shows a **sustainability score of 78.9 (Grade B)** for the combined entity. This indicates strong underlying earnings quality with manageable risks.

Key strengths include **perfect customer concentration scores** — no single client exceeds concentration thresholds, and the HHI of 52.6 shows well-distributed revenue. **Adjustment stability** is also strong with all 20 EBITDA adjustments fully supported by documentation.

**Contract duration** is the main concern at 63.2 points. While average client tenure is solid at 8.2 years, **83% of revenue is non-recurring** (project-based rather than subscription), which creates earnings volatility. This is typical for consulting but impacts the sustainability.

**Working capital health** scores 80 points with stable DSO at 13.5 days and improving trends. **Margin stability** is strong at 90 points, with EBITDA margins trending upward from 18.2% to 18.5%.

The **organic revenue component** scores lowest at 25.8 due to the high proportion of acquisition-driven growth in both entities' histories.

The portal is now showing the detailed QoE tracking with quarterly projections through 2026. This B-grade sustainability supports the EBITDA bridge assumptions and gives confidence in the $1.86B steady-state target.

Any specific sustainability factors you want to drill into?
